relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The judgments are placed before the freed people.
Servitude is limited by release in the seventh year.
Household status shapes departure in the ancient law.
The text exposes the pain of ownership systems around family.
Love and bondage become entangled in the servant's choice.
The doorpost bears the mark of permanent service.
A daughter sold as servant enters a different and troubling protection-law.
Treacherous treatment blocks foreign sale and requires redemption.
If designated for a son, she receives daughter-right.
Food, clothing, and marital rights must not be diminished.
If protection fails, she goes free without payment.
Life-taking is judged with utmost severity.
Unplanned killing receives a place of refuge.
Premeditated killing cannot hide at the altar.
Violence against parents is treated as grave rupture.
Human stealing is condemned with full severity.
Cursing parents is treated as grave dishonor.
Injury from quarrel creates accountability.
Lost time and healing must be paid.
Servant death under violence demands avenging.
The text preserves a troubling ownership logic needing ethical audit.
Pregnancy harm from fighting requires judicial reckoning.
If harm follows, proportional justice is invoked.
Eye for eye limits retaliation to proportion.
Wound answers wound under measured justice.
A servant injured in the eye goes free.
A servant injured in the tooth goes free.
A dangerous animal that kills is removed.
Known danger ignored becomes owner accountability.
Ransom may redeem life under judgment.
Sons and daughters are covered by the same judgment.
Servant death receives compensation in the ancient system.
An uncovered pit creates liability.
The pit owner restores loss.
Shared loss is divided when fault is unclear.
Known danger requires full repayment.

contemplative_note: Exodus 21 is ancient law in a wounded world: it limits violence, demands restitution, exposes ownership's pain, and must be carried forward only through liberation and human dignity.
